issue: Create a PR to sync up juzar/ai-os repo to local project
solution:
# ANALYSIS

## 1. WHAT THIS PROJECT/SYSTEM IS
AI-OS V4 - Multi-model AI DevOps automation platform with GitHub integration, Kubernetes diagnostics, and infrastructure management capabilities.

---

## 2. KEY COMPONENTS

| Component | Purpose | Status |
|-----------|---------|--------|
| `ai/main.py` | CLI entry point (devops, incident, kube, perplexity modes) | Core |
| `ai/executor.py` | LLM calls (Claude, OpenAI) + memory retrieval | Core |
| `ai/agents/kubernetes.py` | kubectl wrappers (pod analysis, logs, describe) | Active |
| `ai/agents/github.py` + `github_writer.py` | GitHub PR review/write operations | Active |
| `ai/agents/infra.py` | Azure/AWS CLI wrappers | Active |
| `memory.json` / `memory_store/` | Persistent context storage (202KB base + session files) | Active |
| `sessions/active.json` | Current session state (67KB) | Active |
| `.env` | API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub token) | Required |

---

## 3. ISSUES / RISKS

| Issue | Severity | Details |
|-------|----------|---------|
| **Hardcoded subprocess shells** | HIGH | `ai/agents/infra.py`, `kubernetes.py`, `executor.py` use `shell=True` without input validation → command injection risk |
| **No API rate limiting** | HIGH | LLM calls in `executor.py` lack throttling/retry logic |
| **Credentials in .env** | HIGH |
